---
title: "Highlights"
author:
  - name: "Jacob T. Dingwall"
date: ""
format:
  docx:
    reference-doc: mer_v7_times_new_roman_reference.docx
    toc: false
    number-sections: false
  html:
    css: mer_v7_print.css
    number-sections: false
execute:
  enabled: false
lang: en
---

- Bird near/reference contrasts rose after recorded herring spawn onset
- Reported flock size responded more often than checklist detection
- Gulls and sea ducks rose; Ring-billed Gull fell in both components
- Positive effects came after onset, not in the 14 days before it
- A comparator taxon also rose, limiting causal interpretation
